linear_context: Filed as a fast-follow finding from SLM-242 (RSC-A06)'s audit; suggested scope was to fix the recursive-depth-supervision per-depth weighting defect
note: SLM-277's underlying defect (per-depth weight w summed into the zero-guard total but never multiplied into the per-depth loss term) does not reproduce on main (commit 91edcae). It was already fixed by the independent SLM-237 (RSC-A01) lineage, committed as part of ada0081 'E626-E631 decode-margin lineage + SLM-237/238/239 recursive-denoiser work (#625)', which predates SLM-277's filing. SLM-277 was filed from an audit of PR #660's branch stack (base claude/great-dirac-0wieip @ eb60c600), which forked before ada0081 landed on main and therefore still carries the historical, unfixed formula. This is fleet duplication across independently-forked agent branches, not a live defect on main.

fixture_rerun_on_main.conclusion: recorded loss matches the weighted formula (rel diff < 1e-5), not the defective unweighted formula (diff of ~11.84) -- the fix from SLM-237/RSC-A01 is live on main

branch_provenance_check.pr_660_base_code_at_defect_site: total_w = sum(ds_weights[:usable])  -- the w-unused historical formula, confirmed present via git show

recommendation.linear: Close SLM-277 as already-resolved-by-different-lineage (cite ada0081 / iter-rsc-a01-depth-supervision-weighting-fix-20260721.md), not as a duplicate to re-implement.
recommendation.github_pr_660: Stale relative to main for this specific finding: its own body reports the bug as 'deliberately left unfixed', but main fixed it via a parallel branch before PR #660 opened. PR #660's own schedule-validation contribution (harness_core/schedule_validation.py + twotower_schedule_policy.py) may still be independently valuable and non-duplicative of main's SLM-242 implementation (twotower_numeric_gates.py) -- that overlap needs owner triage, not an automated close, since the two implementations differ in shape.
recommendation.fleet_coordination: Multiple concurrent claude/great-dirac-* branches are independently re-solving the same Linear ticket numbers (two independent SLM-242 implementations observed: 91edcae on main vs PR #660). Left for the operator: no automated action taken beyond this evidence record and the Linear comment described above.

non_goals: No code change (nothing to fix on main)
No re-running of SLM-233's matched recursive-depth campaign (its own scope, contingent on a real fix landing, which already happened via SLM-237)
No decision on PR #660's disposition (owner/human call)
